articles.title: Odisha sizzles under extreme heat; five districts extend summer vacation for schools
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds") Odisha
articles.full_text:
Bhubaneswar, Jun 17 (PTI) Odisha sizzled under extreme heat on Wednesday with Boudh in the western part of the state registering a maximum temperature of 42.7 degrees Celsius, forcing five districts to extend the summer vacation in schools and anganwadi centres.
Schools in Bolangir, Sonepur, Ganjam, Angul and Sundargarh will remain closed till June 20, instead of June 18. With June 21 being a Sunday, schools in these districts will reopen on June 22, officials said.
In Nuapada district, classes will be held from 6.30 am to 11.30 between June 18 and June 20, according to an order issued by the district education officer.
Schools have remained closed since April 27 for summer vacation this year, following a rise in temperature across various districts.
On Wednesday, Sambalpur recorded a high of 41.5 degrees Celsius, neighbouring Hirakud 41.4 degrees Celsius, Jharsuguda 40.8 degrees Celsius, Sonepur 40.1 degrees Celsius, and Titlagarh 40 degrees Celsius.
The India Meteorological Department’s Bhubaneswar Centre said heatwave conditions are expected to prevail till June 21. PTI BBM BBM NSD
This report is auto-generated from PTI news service. ThePrint holds no responsibility for its content.

articles.title: Odisha extends summer holidays in several districts due to heatwave, morning classes for Nuapada - Odisha TV
articles.full_text:
The continuing heatwave across several parts of Odisha has prompted district administrations to take precautionary measures to protect students from extreme weather conditions. While some districts have decided to extend summer vacations, others have opted for revised school timings to minimise exposure to the scorching heat.
In Angul district, the summer vacation has been extended till June 20, with schools scheduled to reopen on June 22. The district administration said the decision was taken in view of the prevailing heatwave conditions and concerns over student safety.
A similar decision has been taken in Subarnapur district, where schools and colleges will remain closed until June 20.
The district administration has also ordered all Anganwadi centres to remain shut during this period. Authorities clarified that if any examinations are scheduled, they will be conducted during morning hours to avoid the intense afternoon heat.
Angul, Subarnapur and Ganjam Extend Vacation
The extension of summer holidays comes as several districts continue to experience high temperatures and heatwave conditions.
In Ganjam district, authorities have also extended the summer vacation till June 20 for students up to Class XII. Schools in the district are expected to reopen on June 22.
District officials have advised educational institutions to remain vigilant and follow all necessary guidelines issued in the interest of students' health and well-being.
Morning schools in Bolangir
Morning school timings will be implemented in Bolangir district from tomorrow.The decision has been taken by the District Collector in view of the intense heatwave conditions. Classes will be conducted from 6:30 AM to 10:30 AM.
Nuapada Opts for Morning Classes
Unlike other districts that have extended vacations, Nuapada has decided to continue academic activities with revised timings. The District Education Department has directed schools to conduct morning classes from June 18 to June 20.
As per the order, schools will function from 6:30 AM to 11:30 AM, allowing students to complete classes before temperatures rise significantly later in the day. The move is aimed at ensuring academic continuity while safeguarding students from the adverse effects of the ongoing heatwave.

articles.title: Summer Vacation Extended In Angul District Due To Heatwave - Ommcom News
articles.full_text:
Bhubaneswar: The district administration has extended summer vacation for schools in view of the prevailing heatwave conditions.
Government and private schools in Angul will now reopen on June 22 instead of the earlier scheduled date of June 18.
Angul District Education Officer informed that the decision was taken after assessing the ongoing heatwave situation in the region to safeguard the health and well-being of students.The state government had initially announced June 18 as the reopening date for schools across Odisha.
However, considering the intense heat conditions, district Collectors have been empowered to take appropriate decisions regarding school reopening in their respective districts.This move comes as several parts of the state continue to experience scorching temperatures, with meteorologists forecasting no immediate relief from the heatwave in the coming days.
Parents and students have welcomed the extension, citing concerns over children travelling to school and attending classes during peak afternoon heat. School authorities have been directed to ensure all necessary arrangements are in place for smooth functioning from June 22.

articles.title: Odisha dists extend summer vacation amid intense heatwave - Sambad English
articles.full_text:
Bhubaneswar: At least seven districts in Odisha have extended the summer vacation for schools in view of the prevailing hot and humid weather conditions.
The authorities in Ganjam, Sundargarh, Sonepur, Angul, Boudh, Balangir and Sambalpur have extended the summer vacation due to the adverse weather conditions.
Earlier, the state government had announced that the schools across Odisha will reopen on June 18 after the summer vacation, that began on April 27.
The Ganjam Collector and District Magistrate extended the summer vacation till June 20 by issuing an office order today.
“The MeT Department has predicted hot and humid weather conditions as well as thunderstorms for Ganjam district during the next few days. In the interest of public safety and to avert any health casualties, all government, government-aided and private schools up to Class-XII in Ganjam will remain closed till June 20,” said the office order issued by the Ganjam Collector.
The administration in Sundargarh has extended the summer vacation for schools up to Class-XII till June 21.
It is worth mentioning here that the authorities in Sonepur and Angul have also extended the summer vacation for schools due to the prevailing adverse weather conditions.
The summer vacation in Sonepur has been extended till June 20 while schools in Angul district will remain closed till June 22.
Similarly, the Collectors of Boudh and Balangir have extended the summer vacation for educational institutions, including Anganwadi Centres, schools and colleges, till June 20.
The administration in Sambalpur district has extended the summer vacation till June 20 due to the prevailing adverse weather conditions as well as for the Sitalsasthi festival, reports said.
The authorities in Nuapada district have asked the schools to hold morning classes till June 20 due to the prevailing hot and humid conditions.
The schools have been asked to hold classes from 6.30 am to 11.30 am till June 20. The Nuapada Collector today issued an order in this regard.
The administration, meanwhile, revealed that schools across Khurda district, including the capital city, will reopen on June 18 (Thursday). The classes will be held from 10 am to 4 pm, said Khurda District Education Officer (DEO) Sonamika Ray.

articles.title: Summer Vacation Extended In Bargarh Due To Prevailing Heatwave - Ommcom News
articles.full_text:
Bhubaneswar: In view of the severe heatwave conditions sweeping across the district, Bargarh District Collector has announced the extension of summer vacation for all schools.
The summer vacation, which was scheduled to end earlier, has now been extended till June 20, 2026. All government and private schools in the district will reopen on Monday, June 22, 2026. June 21 being a Sunday, the reopening has been adjusted accordingly.
The decision was taken by the District Collector after reviewing the prevailing heatwave situation to safeguard the health and well-being of students from extreme temperatures.
With this announcement, Bargarh has joined Sonepur and Angul districts, where the respective District Collectors had earlier extended summer vacations in their districts due to the intense heatwave conditions.
The district administration urged parents and students to take necessary precautions against the heat until conditions improve.
